result:
All changes are implemented and verified. Here's a summary of what was done:

## Changes Made

### Root Cause
The `SpacingAroundRangeOperatorRule` only checked for the `RANGE` element type (the `..` operator) and did not handle the `RANGE_UNTIL` element type (the `..<` operator introduced in Kotlin 1.7.20+).

### Files Modified

1. **`ktlint-rule-engine-core/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/core/api/ElementType.kt`** (line 216)
   - Added `public val RANGE_UNTIL: IElementType = KtTokens.RANGE_UNTIL` alongside the existing `RANGE` definition

2. **`ktlint-core/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/core/ast/ElementType.kt`** (line 220)
   - Added the same `RANGE_UNTIL` definition to the deprecated backward-compatibility module

3. **`ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/SpacingAroundRangeOperatorRule.kt`**
   - Added import for `ElementType.RANGE_UNTIL`
   - Changed the condition from `node.elementType == RANGE` to `node.elementType == RANGE || node.elementType == RANGE_UNTIL`
   - Changed hardcoded `".."` strings in error messages to use `node.text` dynamically, so the error message correctly shows `..<` for the range-until operator and `..` for the range-to operator

### Behavior
With these changes, the rule now:
- Detects and removes unwanted spacing before and after both `..` and `..<` operators
- Reports accurate error messages (e.g., `Unexpected spacing around "..<"` vs `Unexpected spacing around ".."`)
- Supports auto-correction for both operators
